commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can broaden the stack-frame guard and tests on the PR branch without changing the product direction.; Needs attention: The diff still has a concrete security-boundary concern because common stack-frame path forms can bypass the new unsafe multiline guard and be appended to user-visible replies.; Review finding: [P1] Recognize colon-bearing stack frames before appending suffixes â€” src... (sha=5770b2719f8911daf0015e9f3d234f65d827e4d9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates `sanitizeUserFacingText` to classify a leading error-prefix line, append safe multiline follow-up prose, strip raw stack/JSON/HTML suffixes, and add focused sanitizer tests.

PR surface: Source +76, Tests +148. Total +224 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the shared sanitizer source path: current main sends error-stop assistant text through `sanitizeUserFacingText` with `errorContext: true`, and leading error-prefixed payloads are rewritten before delivery. No live Discord roundtrip was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can broaden the stack-frame guard and tests on the PR branch without changing the product direction.

target.title: fix(sanitize): preserve user-visible prose after error-prefix lines
target.body:
## Summary

When an assistant message stopped with `stopReason="error"` and the visible text started with an error prefix (e.g. `"Error:"`, `"request failed:"`), `sanitizeUserFacingText` replaced the whole payload with a templated error label. Any user-visible prose that followed the error line â€” recommendations, next steps, commands to try â€” was silently dropped from the channel reply.

- **Problem**: `sanitizeUserFacingText` error-context path rewrites the entire text when `ERROR_PREFIX_RE` matches, discarding multi-line prose that follows the error prefix
- **Solution**: Classify the leading error line through existing safe formatters, then append only safe follow-up prose â€” skipping raw stack frames, JSON payload bodies, and HTML error pages
- **What changed**: `sanitize-user-facing-text.ts` (+76 lines: `isUnsafeMultilineErrorBody` guard, `classifyErrorPrefixLine` helper, multi-line suffix detection in `errorContext` branch) + `sanitize-user-facing-text.test.ts` (new, 148 lines, 11 tests)
- **What did NOT change**: Single-line error fragments still flow into the existing rewrite logic unchanged; `errorContext: false` path is unaffected; no changes to callers or upstream text assembly

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #96007
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

When a Discord bot sends a multi-part reply and one section contains an inline tool error, users only see the error line â€” all subsequent recommendations and next steps are silently dropped. This makes it impossible for users to see the full agent response. The root cause is in `sanitizeUserFacingText`, which is channel-agnostic and affects all channel types, not just Discord.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: Assistant reply text containing an error-prefix line followed by multi-line user-visible prose was replaced entirely with a templated error label, losing recommendations/next steps. Additionally, raw stack frames and JSON error payloads could leak into channel replies.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node 24.13.1, branch `fix/sanitize-error-context-96007`, commit `5770b27`

- **Evidence after fix (inline probe):**

```console
$ node --import tsx -e '
import { sanitizeUserFacingText } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.js";

// 1. timeout + recommendations (multi-line prose preserved)
const r1 = sanitizeUserFacingText(
  "Error: connection timed out\n\nRecommendation: try restarting the service.\nNext steps:\n1. Check logs\n2. Retry",
  { errorContext: true }
);
console.log("Test 1 - Has Recommendation:", r1.includes("Recommendation"));
console.log("Test 1 - Has timed out:", r1.includes("timed out"));

// 2. transport error + prose
const r2 = sanitizeUserFacingText(
  "Error: fetch failed. SocketError: other side closed\n\nHere is a summary of findings:\n  Item A is healthy\n  Item B is healthy\n\nRecommendation: try restarting.",
  { errorContext: true }
);
console.log("Test 2 - Has summary:", r2.includes("summary of findings"));
console.log("Test 2 - Has LLM request failed:", r2.includes("LLM request failed"));
'
Test 1 - Has Recommendation: true
Test 1 - Has timed out: true
Test 2 - Has summary: true
Test 2 - Has LLM request failed: true
```

- **Evidence after fix (stack/JSON/HTML guards):**

```console
// 3. Stack trace suffix â€” MUST NOT leak
$ node --import tsx -e '
const r = sanitizeUserFacingText(
  "Error: ECONNREFUSED\n    at Server.onError (server.ts:42:8)\n    at emitError (events.js:153:12)\n    at processTicksAndRejections (internal/process/task_queues.js:95:5)",
  { errorContext: true }
);
console.log("server.ts leaked:", r.includes("server.ts"));
console.log("events.js leaked:", r.includes("events.js"));
console.log("Has connection refused:", r.includes("connection refused"));
'
server.ts leaked: false
events.js leaked: false
Has connection refused: true

// 4. JSON payload body â€” MUST NOT leak
$ node --import tsx -e '
const r = sanitizeUserFacingText(
  "Error: fetch failed\n{\"error\":{\"type\":\"server_error\",\"message\":\"internal\"}}\n{\"request_id\":\"req_abc123\"}",
  { errorContext: true }
);
console.log("server_error leaked:", r.includes("server_error"));
console.log("req_abc123 leaked:", r.includes("req_abc123"));
'
server_error leaked: false
req_abc123 leaked: false

// 5. HTML error page â€” MUST NOT leak
$ node --import tsx -e '
const r = sanitizeUserFacingText(
  "Error: gateway error\n<!doctype html>\n<html><body>502 Bad Gateway</body></html>",
  { errorContext: true }
);
console.log("DOCTYPE leaked:", r.includes("<!doctype html>"));
console.log("502 leaked:", r.includes("502 Bad Gateway"));
'
DOCTYPE leaked: false
502 leaked: false

// 6. Single-line â€” still rewritten
$ node --import tsx -e 'console.log(sanitizeUserFacingText("Error: connection timed out", { errorContext: true }));'
LLM request timed out.

// 7. errorContext: false â€” passthrough
$ node --import tsx -e 'console.log(sanitizeUserFacingText("Error: timeout\n\nReco: retry", { errorContext: false }));'
Error: timeout

Reco: retry
```

- **Evidence after fix (full test suite):**

```console
$ pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 âś“ classifies timeout error line and appends recommendations
 âś“ classifies transport error line and appends safe multi-paragraph content
 âś“ classifies leading line and appends guidance after an API Error prefix
 âś“ returns only the classified error when suffix is a stack trace
 âś“ returns only the classified error when suffix is a JSON payload body
 âś“ returns only the classified error when suffix is an HTML error page
 âś“ appends safe prose after a classified leading line even when one line looks suspicious
 âś“ rewrites a bare Error: timeout line
 âś“ rewrites a bare transport error line (econnrefused)
 âś“ rewrites a transport error line without suffix content
 âś“ passes through full text when errorContext is false

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  11 passed (11)
   Start at  11:50:08
   Duration  527ms
```

**Before/after matrix**:

| Input | `errorContext` | Before fix | After fix |
|-------|---------------|-----------|----------|
| `Error: timeout\n\nReco: retry` | `true` | `LLM request timed out.` | classified error + prose preserved |
| `Error: Connection refused\n\nTry again` | `true` | `LLM request failed: connection refused...` | classified error + prose preserved |
| `request failed: conn reset\n\nSteps: 1. Check` | `true` | `LLM request failed: network connection was interrupted.` | classified error + prose preserved |
| `Error: ECONNREFUSED\n    at Server.onError (server.ts:42:8)\n    at emitError (...)` | `true` | `LLM request failed: connection refused...` | classified error, stack frames stripped |
| `Error: fetch failed\n{"error":{...}}` | `true` | `LLM request failed: network connection error.` | classified error, JSON body stripped |
| `Error: gateway error\n<!doctype html>...` | `true` | generic error label | classified error, HTML body stripped |
| `Error: timeout` (single line) | `true` | `LLM request timed out.` | `LLM request timed out.` (unchanged) |
| `Error: ECONNREFUSED` (single line) | `true` | `LLM request failed: connection refused...` | unchanged |
| Any text | `false` | Full text preserved | unchanged |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. Multi-line prose after a classified error-prefix line is preserved in channel replies
  2. Stack traces (2+ stack-frame lines), JSON error payload bodies, and HTML error pages are stripped
  3. Single-line error fragments still produce the existing templated error labels
  4. Non-error context path (`errorContext: false`) is completely unaffected
  5. All 11 regression tests pass covering: prose preservation, stack/JSON/HTML stripping, single-line rewrite, and errorContext passthrough

- **What was not tested**: Live Discord roundtrip (requires a real Discord bot token and account); behavior on non-Linux platforms

## Root Cause

In `sanitizeUserFacingText`, when `errorContext` is `true` and the trimmed text matches `ERROR_PREFIX_RE`, the function unconditionally rewrote the entire payload into a short templated error label. It did not check whether the text contains substantive multi-line prose after the error prefix that should be preserved, nor did it guard against raw stack frames and error payload bodies leaking through.

The `errorContext: true` path is activated by `finalizeAssistantExtraction` in `embedded-agent-utils.ts:47-49` whenever `msg.stopReason === "error"`.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Channel replies that contain an error-prefix line followed by user-visible prose (e.g. recommendations, next steps) will now show the prose content after the error prefix, rather than being replaced entirely by a short error label. Raw stack frames, JSON error bodies, and HTML error pages are stripped from the suffix.

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No
- [x] Stack trace / internal path leakage addressed? Yes â€” `isUnsafeMultilineErrorBody` strips 2+ stack-frame suffixes and JSON/HTML bodies before appending prose

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: Multi-line error text preservation, stack trace stripping, JSON body stripping, HTML body stripping, single-line error rewrite, non-error-context passthrough
- **Edge cases checked**: Empty error prefix, empty suffix, single suspicious line (not enough to trigger stack detection), text with only the error prefix and no content after
- **What you did NOT verify**: Live Discord delivery, multi-channel behavior (Telegram/Slack/WhatsApp), interaction with streaming/progress drafts

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [x] Config/env changes? No
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. The guard is placed at the exact rewrite point inside `sanitizeUserFacingText`, which is the single funnel for all user-facing error text across all channels.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The existing error-rewrite logic is well-structured; the added helpers (`isUnsafeMultilineErrorBody`, `classifyErrorPrefixLine`) are narrow and self-contained.
- **Alternative considered**: Adding channel-specific guards in Discord's outbound adapter. Rejected because this is a text-sanitization problem, not a delivery problem â€” the fix benefits all channels uniformly.

## AI Assistance
target.author: jiashu6
